---
title: "Warm-up: mixed review"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-copy: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    highlight-style: github
---

## Goal

- Self-test on **all** the warm-up skills mixed together, in random order.
- Practise picking the right rule for each problem without being told which.
- Build confidence with small numbers and clean answers before biology starts.

## The method

This page is a grab-bag. Each drill uses one of the skills from the earlier
warm-ups. A quick reminder of the toolbox:

- **Dimensions.** An $m\times n$ matrix has $m$ rows and $n$ columns.
- **Addition / scalar.** Add matrices entrywise (same size); a scalar multiplies
  every entry.
- **Matrix × vector.** Each output entry is a row dotted with the vector.
- **Matrix × matrix.** Entry $(i,j)$ is row $i$ of the left dotted with column
  $j$ of the right.
- **Transpose.** $A^\top$ turns rows into columns.
- **Determinant.** $\det\begin{bmatrix} a & b \\ c & d \end{bmatrix} = ad - bc$.
- **2×2 inverse.** $A^{-1} = \frac{1}{ad-bc}\begin{bmatrix} d & -b \\ -c & a \end{bmatrix}$.
- **Solve a 2×2 system.** $\mathbf{x} = A^{-1}\mathbf{b}$.

**Tiny worked example (matrix × vector).**
$$
\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ 0 & 3 \end{bmatrix}
\begin{bmatrix} 4 \\ 1 \end{bmatrix}
= \begin{bmatrix} (1)(4)+(2)(1) \\ (0)(4)+(3)(1) \end{bmatrix}
= \begin{bmatrix} 6 \\ 3 \end{bmatrix}.
$$

## Drills

Do them in order; resist peeking at the solutions.

1. **(Dimensions)** How many rows and columns does
$\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 4 & 5 & 6 \end{bmatrix}$ have?

2. **(Addition)** Compute
$\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 4 \end{bmatrix} + \begin{bmatrix} 5 & 0 \\ 1 & 2 \end{bmatrix}.$

3. **(Determinant)** $\det\begin{bmatrix} 3 & 2 \\ 1 & 4 \end{bmatrix}.$

4. **(Scalar)** Compute
$3\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 0 \\ 2 & 1 \end{bmatrix}.$

5. **(Transpose)** Write the transpose of
$\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 4 \end{bmatrix}.$

6. **(Matrix × vector)** Compute
$\begin{bmatrix} 2 & 0 \\ 1 & 3 \end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix} 1 \\ 2 \end{bmatrix}.$

7. **(2×2 inverse)** Find the inverse of
$\begin{bmatrix} 2 & 1 \\ 1 & 1 \end{bmatrix}.$

8. **(Transpose)** Write the transpose of
$\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 4 & 5 & 6 \end{bmatrix}.$

9. **(Is det zero?)** $\det\begin{bmatrix} 2 & 4 \\ 1 & 2 \end{bmatrix}$ — is it $0$?

10. **(Matrix × matrix)** Compute
$\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix} 5 & 6 \\ 7 & 8 \end{bmatrix}.$

11. **(Matrix × matrix)** Compute
$\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix} 3 & 0 \\ 1 & 4 \end{bmatrix}.$

12. **(Solve a system)** Solve $A\mathbf{x} = \mathbf{b}$ with
$A = \begin{bmatrix} 2 & 1 \\ 1 & 1 \end{bmatrix}$, $\mathbf{b} = \begin{bmatrix} 5 \\ 3 \end{bmatrix}$.

13. **(Matrix × vector)** Compute
$\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 \end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix} 2 \\ 1 \end{bmatrix}.$

14. **(Determinant, 3×3)** $\det\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 0 & 2 \\ 0 & 3 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}.$

15. **(2×2 inverse)** Find the inverse of
$\begin{bmatrix} 4 & 2 \\ 1 & 1 \end{bmatrix}.$ (determinant $2$)

16. **(Solve a system)** Solve $A\mathbf{x} = \mathbf{b}$ with
$A = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 4 \end{bmatrix}$, $\mathbf{b} = \begin{bmatrix} 5 \\ 11 \end{bmatrix}$.

## Check in R

Build matrices with [`matrix`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/matrix.html), multiply
with `%*%`, transpose with [`t`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/t.html), take
determinants with [`det`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/det.html), and invert or solve
with [`solve`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/solve.html).

```r
A <- matrix(c(1, 2,
              3, 4), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)
dim(A)
# [1] 2 2

t(A)
#      [,1] [,2]
# [1,]    1    3
# [2,]    2    4

det(matrix(c(3, 2, 1, 4), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE))
# [1] 10

solve(matrix(c(2, 1, 1, 1), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE), c(5, 3))
# [1] 2 1            (x1 = 2, x2 = 1)
```

## Hints

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Hint 1 — which rule?"}
Read the label in bold before each drill — it names the skill. Then recall the
matching one-line rule from "The method".
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Hint 2 — products line up rows with columns"}
For matrix×vector and matrix×matrix, the output entry is "row of the left
$\cdot$ column of the right": multiply matching entries and add.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Hint 3 — transpose"}
The first **row** becomes the first **column**, the second row becomes the
second column, and so on. An $m\times n$ matrix becomes $n\times m$.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Hint 4 — solving a system"}
Get $A^{-1}$ with the $2\times2$ formula, then $\mathbf{x} = A^{-1}\mathbf{b}$
is a matrix-times-vector product.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Hint 5 — 3×3 determinant with zeros"}
Expand along the row (or column) with the most zeros to kill terms early. The
signs along the top row are $+,\,-,\,+$.
:::

## Step-by-step solutions

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Solutions"}

**Drill 1.** It has **2 rows and 3 columns**, so it is a $2\times3$ matrix.

**Drill 2.** Add entrywise:
$$
\begin{bmatrix} 1+5 & 2+0 \\ 3+1 & 4+2 \end{bmatrix}
= \begin{bmatrix} 6 & 2 \\ 4 & 6 \end{bmatrix}.
$$

**Drill 3.** $(3)(4) - (2)(1) = 12 - 2 = 10.$

**Drill 4.** Multiply every entry by $3$:
$$
\begin{bmatrix} 3(1) & 3(0) \\ 3(2) & 3(1) \end{bmatrix}
= \begin{bmatrix} 3 & 0 \\ 6 & 3 \end{bmatrix}.
$$

**Drill 5.** Rows become columns:
$$
\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 4 \end{bmatrix}^\top
= \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 3 \\ 2 & 4 \end{bmatrix}.
$$

**Drill 6.**
$$
\begin{bmatrix} (2)(1)+(0)(2) \\ (1)(1)+(3)(2) \end{bmatrix}
= \begin{bmatrix} 2 \\ 7 \end{bmatrix}.
$$

**Drill 7.** $\det = (2)(1)-(1)(1) = 1$.
$A^{-1} = \frac{1}{1}\begin{bmatrix} 1 & -1 \\ -1 & 2 \end{bmatrix}
= \begin{bmatrix} 1 & -1 \\ -1 & 2 \end{bmatrix}.$

**Drill 8.** The $2\times3$ becomes $3\times2$:
$$
\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 4 & 5 & 6 \end{bmatrix}^\top
= \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 4 \\ 2 & 5 \\ 3 & 6 \end{bmatrix}.
$$

**Drill 9.** $(2)(2)-(4)(1) = 4 - 4 = 0.$ **Yes**, the determinant is $0$
(second row is half the first).

**Drill 10.** Multiplying by the identity returns the other matrix unchanged:
$$
\begin{bmatrix} 5 & 6 \\ 7 & 8 \end{bmatrix}.
$$
(Check one entry: row 1 of $I$ dotted with column 1 is $(1)(5)+(0)(7)=5$.)

**Drill 11.**
$$
\begin{bmatrix}
(1)(3)+(2)(1) & (1)(0)+(2)(4) \\
(0)(3)+(1)(1) & (0)(0)+(1)(4)
\end{bmatrix}
= \begin{bmatrix} 5 & 8 \\ 1 & 4 \end{bmatrix}.
$$

**Drill 12.** $\det = (2)(1)-(1)(1) = 1$, so
$A^{-1} = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & -1 \\ -1 & 2 \end{bmatrix}$. Then
$$
\mathbf{x} = A^{-1}\mathbf{b}
= \begin{bmatrix} 1 & -1 \\ -1 & 2 \end{bmatrix}
\begin{bmatrix} 5 \\ 3 \end{bmatrix}
= \begin{bmatrix} (1)(5)+(-1)(3) \\ (-1)(5)+(2)(3) \end{bmatrix}
= \begin{bmatrix} 2 \\ 1 \end{bmatrix}.
$$
So $x_1 = 2,\ x_2 = 1$.

**Drill 13.**
$$
\begin{bmatrix} (1)(2)+(1)(1) \\ (2)(2)+(3)(1) \end{bmatrix}
= \begin{bmatrix} 3 \\ 7 \end{bmatrix}.
$$

**Drill 14.** Expand along the top row $1, 0, 2$ (the $0$ kills its term):
$$
1\det\begin{bmatrix} 3 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}
- 0
+ 2\det\begin{bmatrix} 0 & 3 \\ 1 & 0 \end{bmatrix}.
$$
First minor: $(3)(1)-(0)(0) = 3$. Third minor: $(0)(0)-(3)(1) = -3$.
So $1(3) - 0 + 2(-3) = 3 - 6 = -3.$

**Drill 15.** $\det = (4)(1)-(2)(1) = 2$.
$A^{-1} = \frac{1}{2}\begin{bmatrix} 1 & -2 \\ -1 & 4 \end{bmatrix}
= \begin{bmatrix} \tfrac{1}{2} & -1 \\ -\tfrac{1}{2} & 2 \end{bmatrix}.$

**Drill 16.** $\det = (1)(4)-(2)(3) = 4 - 6 = -2$, so
$A^{-1} = \frac{1}{-2}\begin{bmatrix} 4 & -2 \\ -3 & 1 \end{bmatrix}
= \begin{bmatrix} -2 & 1 \\ \tfrac{3}{2} & -\tfrac{1}{2} \end{bmatrix}.$
Then
$$
\mathbf{x} = A^{-1}\mathbf{b}
= \begin{bmatrix} -2 & 1 \\ \tfrac{3}{2} & -\tfrac{1}{2} \end{bmatrix}
\begin{bmatrix} 5 \\ 11 \end{bmatrix}
= \begin{bmatrix} (-2)(5)+(1)(11) \\ (\tfrac{3}{2})(5)+(-\tfrac{1}{2})(11) \end{bmatrix}
= \begin{bmatrix} 1 \\ 2 \end{bmatrix}.
$$
So $x_1 = 1,\ x_2 = 2$. (Check: $1 + 2(2) = 5$ and $3(1) + 4(2) = 11$. Both
hold.)

:::
